meaning: vt.将手掌转向下,使伏身,使俯身vi.俯身,伏身
examples: It depends how much you weigh and how much you pronate.
This is especially true to take the pressure off of your knees if you pronate, e. g., ride the inside edge of your foot when you step down.
Running on a road that is banked at the sides, for example, effectively gives you one short leg, causing it to pronate and put pressure on the knee.
But since I pronate, if I was not in neutralizing boots, I would already have artificially more pressure on the left edge of the outside (right) ski and not enough on the left edge of the inside ski.
